title: 10 best herbal teas for oily hair
intro_desc: Herbal teas for oily hair are infusions made from various herbs, which can help regulate sebum production and improve the overall condition of oily hair. These teas often contain natural astringents and anti-inflammatory properties that can gently cleanse and soothe the scalp, reducing excess oiliness. Some examples of herbal teas beneficial for oily hair include green tea, chamomile, sage, and nettle. By incorporating these herbal teas into hair care routines, individuals can experience a reduction in greasiness, improved scalp health, and increased hair volume, leading to a noticeable improvement in their confidence and self-esteem.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with oily hair because it has antiseptic and astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production on the scalp. The tea's natural components, such as rosmarinic acid and eugenol, can help to cleanse and purify the scalp, reducing the excess oil that can lead to greasy-looking hair. Additionally, lemon balm tea contains antioxidants that can help to protect the hair follicles from damage, promoting healthy hair growth. Regular use of lemon balm tea as a hair rinse can help to balance the scalp's natural oil production, leaving hair looking clean, fresh, and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Lemon balm tea helps to remove excess oil from the scalp and hair, due to its tannin content which has astringent properties.
Antibacterial: It helps to maintain a healthy scalp by preventing the growth of bacteria and fungi, thanks to its rosmarinic acid content which has antibacterial and antifungal properties.
Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tea can help to reduce inflammation and irritation on the scalp, caused by excess oil production, due to its eugenol content which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Antioxidant: It helps to protect the hair and scalp from damage caused by free radicals, due to its high content of rosmarinic acid and other antioxidant compounds.

Regulates sebum production: Lemon balm tea can help to regulate sebum production on the scalp, preventing the overproduction of oil, due to its polyphenol content which has been shown to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that contributes to sebum production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have astringent properties, making them ideal for reducing excess oil in the hair.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe an itchy scalp and reduce oil production.
Seeds: The seeds of lemon balm tea plant contain essential oils that can help regulate sebum production and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried lemon balm leaves, letting it steep for 15-20 minutes.
After steeping, strain the mixture to remove the lemon balm leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Mix the cooled lemon balm tea with 1 cup of warm water.
Rinse your oily hair with the mixture, massaging it into your scalp, and leaving it on for 10-15 minutes before rinsing it out.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with oily hair because it contains high levels of silica, a mineral that can help to regulate the production of sebum, the natural oil produced by the scalp. By regulating sebum production, horsetail tea can help to reduce excess oiliness in the hair, leaving it looking and feeling cleaner and fresher for longer. Additionally, horsetail tea has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to soothe the scalp and promote healthy hair growth. Regular consumption of horsetail tea or use of horsetail tea rinses can help to improve the overall health and appearance of oily hair.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Horsetail tea has astringent properties that help regulate sebum production, reducing excess oil on the scalp. This is due to the high tannin content in horsetail, which helps to tighten and constrict pores.

Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate irritation and inflammation on the scalp, reducing dandruff and itchiness associated with oily hair. These properties come from the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins in horsetail.

Antimicrobial: Horsetail tea has antimicrobial properties that help control the growth of microorganisms on the scalp, reducing the risk of infections and promoting a healthy scalp environment. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids in horsetail.

Antioxidant: Horsetail tea has antioxidant properties that help protect the scalp from free radical damage, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy hair growth. These antioxidant properties are due to the presence of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and sterols in horsetail.

Diuretic: Horsetail tea has diuretic properties that help eliminate excess water from the body, reducing fluid retention and swelling in the scalp. This can help reduce the appearance of oily hair and improve overall scalp health. The diuretic properties of horsetail are attributed to the presence of equisetin and other triterpenes.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail tea made from rhizomes is rich in silica, which can help regulate sebum production and control oily hair.
Stems: The stems of horsetail tea plant are also high in silica, making them effective in reducing excess oil in the hair and scalp.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves contain flavonoids and saponins, which can help cleanse and balance oily hair and scalp.
Seeds: Horsetail seeds are a good source of essential fatty acids, which can help nourish and moisturize oily hair, promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried horsetail herb, simmer for 15 minutes.
After simmering, remove from heat and let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs, pouring the liquid into a clean container.
Shampoo your hair as usual, then pour the cooled horsetail tea over your hair, massaging it into your scalp.
Leave the tea on your hair for 10 minutes, then rinse it out thoroughly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime tea helps with oily hair because it contains high levels of antioxidants and vitamin C, which can help regulate sebum production in the scalp. The tannins present in the tea leaves can also help to remove excess oil and buildup from the hair, leaving it clean and refreshed. Additionally, large-leaved lime tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe an irritated scalp and promote hair growth. By using large-leaved lime tea as a rinse or hair treatment, you can help to balance the natural oils in your hair and keep it looking healthy and shiny.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: The tannins present in large-leaved lime tea act as a natural astringent, helping to remove excess oil from the scalp and reducing the appearance of oily hair.
Antibacterial properties: Large-leaved lime tea has antibacterial properties, which can help to maintain a healthy scalp and reduce the production of excess oil caused by bacterial imbalances.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and other polyphenolic compounds in large-leaved lime tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe an irritated scalp and reduce oil production.
Antioxidant properties: Large-leaved lime tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which can help to protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals and promote healthy hair growth.

Balancing properties: The balanced pH of large-leaved lime tea can help to regulate the natural pH of the scalp, reducing the production of excess oil and promoting a healthy, balanced scalp and hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and known for their astringent properties, lime tree leaves can help regulate sebum production in the scalp, reducing oily hair.
Flowers: Lime tree flowers have mild antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help maintain a healthy scalp and reduce oiliness in hair.
Seeds: Lime tree seeds contain essential fatty acids and nutrients that can nourish the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing oiliness.
Bark: The bark of the lime tree has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and astringent properties, making it beneficial for treating oily hair by regulating sebum production.
remedy_recipe: Boil 4 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried large-leaved lime leaves, letting it simmer for 15 minutes.
Steep 3 tablespoons of dried herbal mix (containing rosemary, nettle, and horsetail) in the simmering water for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Mix in the juice of half a lime and 2 tablespoons of raw honey, stirring until fully dissolved.
Massage the tea into your oily hair and scalp, leaving it on for 20 minutes before rinsing thoroughly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with oily hair because it contains natural antiseptic and astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production on the scalp. These properties can also help to unclog hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing excess oiliness. Thyme tea can be used as a rinse after shampooing or as a hair mask before washing to help balance the scalp's natural oil production. Its antibacterial and antifungal properties can also help to maintain a healthy scalp and prevent issues like dandruff and itchiness. Overall, thyme tea can be a natural and effective solution for those struggling with oily hair.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Thyme tea has antimicrobial properties that help control the growth of Malassezia furfur, a fungus that can cause an overproduction of oils in the scalp. The active constituents responsible for this property are thymol and carvacrol.
Astringent: Thyme tea has astringent properties that help regulate sebum production, reducing the excess oiliness in the hair. This is due to the tannins present in thyme.

Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe an irritated scalp, alleviating any discomfort caused by oily hair. The active compounds responsible for this are flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antioxidant: Thyme tea contains antioxidants that support a healthy scalp and hair follicles, promoting overall hair health. These antioxidants include rosmarinic acid and luteolin.
Circulation-enhancing: Thyme tea can help improve blood circulation in the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing oil buildup. This is due to the rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid content in thyme.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in essential oils and antimicrobial compounds, making them effective in controlling excess oil production in the scalp.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to balance sebum production and soothe irritated scalps.
Stems: Thyme stems have astringent properties that can help regulate oil secretion, promoting healthier and more balanced hair.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried thyme leaves, letting it simmer for 10 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the mixture steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the thyme leaves, reserving the liquid.
Allow the thyme tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
After shampooing, pour the thyme tea over your hair, massaging it into your scalp, then rinse it out.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with oily hair because it is believed to regulate sebum production on the scalp, which can reduce greasiness and shine. Its anti-inflammatory properties may also soothe an itchy or irritated scalp, providing relief for those who experience dandruff or other skin conditions. Additionally, rosemary tea contains antioxidants that can promote hair growth and improve overall hair health. By rinsing your hair with rosemary tea, you can help maintain a balanced scalp and promote stronger, shinier locks.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Rosemary tea helps to regulate sebum production, reducing excess oil on the scalp, due to its tannin content.

Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help maintain a healthy scalp by preventing the growth of bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate oily hair, thanks to compounds like camphor and caffeic acid.
Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary tea can soothe an irritated scalp, reducing inflammation and dandruff caused by excessive oil production, as it contains rosmarinic acid and carnosol.
Circulation stimulant: By increasing blood flow to the scalp, rosemary tea promotes hair growth and helps distribute natural oils more evenly, containing rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid.

Detoxifying: The detoxifying properties of rosemary tea help remove impurities and buildup from hair follicles, allowing for better absorption of nutrients and improved hair health, attributed to its antioxidant content.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the rosemary tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have antimicrobial properties, making them effective in balancing the production of oil in the scalp.
Flowers: The flowers of the rosemary tea plant contain essential oils that help regulate sebum production, promoting healthy and oil-free hair.
Stems: The stems of the rosemary tea plant are a good source of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an irritated scalp and reduce oiliness.
Seeds: The seeds of the rosemary tea plant contain high levels of fatty acids, which can help nourish and strengthen the hair, reducing excess oil production.

Buds: The buds of the rosemary tea plant are rich in phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that can help protect the hair from damage caused by environmental stressors and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried rosemary leaves, reducing heat to a simmer for 15 minutes.
After 15 minutes, remove the pot from heat and let the mixture steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove rosemary leaves, pouring the tea into a glass container.
Allow the rosemary tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Before bedtime, massage the cooled rosemary tea onto your scalp, then rinse thoroughly in the morning.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marjoram tea helps with oily hair because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help regulate sebum production in the scalp. Sebum is an oily substance that can make hair look greasy and unclean. Marjoram tea also has astringent properties that can help tighten pores and hair follicles, reducing the amount of oil that is released onto the scalp. Additionally, marjoram tea contains antioxidants that can improve scalp health and promote hair growth, leaving hair looking and feeling cleaner and healthier. Regular use of marjoram tea as a hair rinse or scalp treatment can help maintain the balance of oil production and keep oily hair at bay.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the marjoram tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have astringent properties, making them ideal for balancing the production of oil in the scalp.
Flowers: The flowers of the marjoram tea plant contain essential oils that help to regulate sebum production and soothe irritated scalps, promoting healthy and oil-free hair.

Seeds: The seeds of the marjoram tea plant are a natural source of antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce scalp inflammation and improve the overall health of the hair.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried marjoram leaves, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
After simmering, strain the tea to remove the marjoram leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Massage the marjoram tea into your scalp and damp hair, then let it sit for 15 minutes before rinsing thoroughly.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Marjoram tea has antiseptic properties that help keep the scalp clean and free of bacteria, reducing excess oil production. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and essential oils.
Astringent: Marjoram tea acts as an astringent, helping to tighten the scalp's pores and reduce excess sebum production. This property is attributed to the tea's high tannin content.

Anti-inflammatory: Marjoram tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe an irritated scalp, reducing inflammation and dandruff caused by excess oil. The active compounds responsible for this property include terpinene-4-ol and α-terpineol.

Circulation-boosting: Marjoram tea promotes circulation in the scalp, helping to distribute natural oils more evenly and reduce excess oil buildup. This property is attributed to the tea's rosmarinic acid content.

Balancing: Marjoram tea has a balancing effect on the scalp's oil production, helping to regulate sebum production and maintain a healthy scalp. The active constituents responsible for this property include various flavonoids and volatile oils.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with oily hair because it contains natural properties that can help regulate sebum production in the scalp. The astringent and antiseptic qualities of sage can help to remove excess oil, unclog pores, and stimulate circulation in the scalp. This can lead to healthier and more balanced hair, reducing the appearance of greasiness. Additionally, sage tea is rich in vitamins and minerals that can nourish the hair and promote hair growth. Regular use of sage tea as a hair rinse or hair mask can help to improve the overall health and appearance of oily hair.
remedy_properties: Sebum regulation: Sage tea helps to regulate sebum production in the scalp, reducing excess oiliness in hair (active constituents

Antibacterial properties: Sage tea has antibacterial properties that can help control the growth of bacteria responsible for causing dandruff and other scalp irritations, often associated with oily hair (active constituents
Astringent properties: The astringent properties of sage tea help to tighten the scalp's pores, reducing the secretion of sebum and leaving hair less oily (active constituents
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage tea's anti-inflammatory properties can alleviate scalp inflammation and irritation, which can contribute to an overproduction of sebum (active constituents
Antioxidant properties: Sage tea's antioxidant properties protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthier hair growth and reducing oiliness (active constituents
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients and antioxidants, sage leaves help regulate oil production in the scalp.
Stems: Containing essential oils, sage stems can help balance sebum levels and promote hair growth.
Buds: Packed with flavonoids and volatile oils, sage buds can reduce excess oil and soothe the scalp.
Seeds: High in vitamins and minerals, sage seeds can help strengthen hair and reduce greasiness in oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water and add two teaspoons of dried sage leaves, letting it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the sage leaves, then let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Mix the sage tea with one cup of warm water and two tablespoons of apple cider vinegar.
After shampooing your hair, pour the mixture over your scalp and massage it gently.
Leave it on for 5-10 minutes, then rinse it thoroughly with warm water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with oily hair because it contains properties that can balance the scalp's natural oil production. The tea's natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties can help unclog hair follicles, soothe an irritated scalp, and reduce excess sebum production. Additionally, lavender is known for its calming and soothing effects, which can help alleviate stress-related hormonal imbalances that can contribute to oily hair. By rinsing your hair with lavender tea, you can help maintain a healthy scalp and promote shiny, manageable hair.
remedy_properties: Sebum regulation: English lavender tea helps regulate sebum production in the scalp, reducing excess oiliness in hair. This is due to the presence of linalyl acetate and linalool, which have anti-inflammatory and balancing properties.

Antimicrobial properties: Lavender tea inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, preventing dandruff and other scalp irritations that can lead to increased oil production. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are linalool, lavandulyl acetate, and cis-ocimene.

Scalp circulation improvement: English lavender tea stimulates blood circulation in the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing oiliness by distributing natural oils more evenly. This is attributed to the essential oil components, including 1,8-cineole and camphor.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Lavender tea soothes inflammation and irritation on the scalp, alleviating itchiness and reducing oil production. The anti-inflammatory properties come from linalyl acetate, linalool, and caryophyllene.

Hair conditioning: English lavender tea helps condition hair, making it appear healthier and less greasy. This is due to the nourishing properties of lavender's polyphenols and flavonoids, which smooth hair cuticles and enhance shine.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in essential oils, the flowers of English lavender tea plant help regulate sebum production in the scalp, reducing oily hair.
Leaves: Containing high levels of tannins, lavender leaves help balance the scalp's natural oils, making them an excellent addition to medicinal tea for oily hair.
Buds: With potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, lavender buds soothe the scalp, alleviate itchiness, and reduce excess oil production in the hair.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried English lavender flowers, letting it simmer for 15 minutes.
After simmering, remove the pot from heat and let it steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the lavender flowers, reserving the liquid.
Allow the lavender tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Pour the cooled lavender tea over your scalp and throughout your hair, massaging it in for a few minutes before rinsing it out.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tea helps with oily hair because it contains antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can help regulate sebum production on the scalp. The plant's active compound, artemisinin, has anti-inflammatory effects that can soothe an irritated scalp, reducing the production of excess oil. Moreover, wormwood tea is rich in antioxidants that can promote hair growth and improve overall hair health. Regular use of wormwood tea as a hair rinse can help balance the scalp's oil production, leaving hair looking cleaner and healthier.
remedy_properties: Antiseborrheic: Wormwood tea helps regulate sebum production in the scalp, reducing excess oiliness in hair. This is due to the presence of active constituents such as azulenes and lactones.
Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood tea soothes inflammation on the scalp, providing relief from itchiness and dandruff caused by oily hair. This property is attributed to the compound called chamazulene.
Astringent: Wormwood tea has a tightening effect on the scalp, helping to reduce oiliness and shrink pores. Tannins present in wormwood are responsible for this astringent property.

Antimicrobial: Wormwood tea inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, preventing infections and maintaining a healthy scalp environment. The active constituents responsible for this property include camphor, cineole, and thujone.
Circulation-boosting: Wormwood tea improves blood flow to the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing oiliness. This is due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which stimulate circulation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients and antioxidants, wormwood leaves help balance scalp oil production and promote hair growth.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, wormwood flowers soothe the scalp and reduce excess oil.
Seeds: High in essential oils, wormwood seeds provide nourishment and help regulate sebum production, reducing oily hair.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried wormwood herb; let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Turn off the heat, cover the pot, and let the mixture steep for an additional 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs, reserving the liquid.
Allow the wormwood tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
After shampooing, pour the wormwood tea over your hair, massaging it into the scalp and letting it sit for 10 minutes before rinsing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silver birch tea helps with oily hair because it contains betulinic acid, a compound known for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. This acid can help regulate sebum production, which is the primary cause of oily hair. Additionally, silver birch tea has astringent properties that can help tighten pores and hair follicles, reducing the amount of oil that is released onto the scalp. The tea also has high levels of vitamin C, which can improve the health and appearance of hair by promoting collagen production and increasing blood flow to the scalp. Drinking silver birch tea or using it as a rinse can help maintain the natural pH balance of the scalp, preventing the overproduction of oil and leaving hair looking clean and healthy.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Silver birch tea has astringent properties that help regulate sebum production, reducing excess oil on the scalp. This is due to the presence of tannins.
Anti-inflammatory: It helps alleviate inflammation caused by buildup on the scalp, promoting a healthier environment for hair growth. This property is attributed to betulinic acid.

Antimicrobial: The tea inhibits the growth of microorganisms that can lead to dandruff and other scalp irritations, contributing to a balanced scalp. The antimicrobial effects are attributed to the organic acids and phenolic compounds.
Detoxifying: Silver birch tea helps remove toxins from the scalp, unclogging hair follicles and promoting healthy hair growth. This is due to the diuretic properties of the birch leaves.

Circulation-boosting: It improves blood circulation in the scalp, ensuring that essential nutrients reach hair follicles for optimal hair health. This is facilitated by the flavonoids and essential oils present in silver birch tea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in plant compounds that can help regulate sebum production in the scalp, reducing oiliness in hair.
Bark: Contains anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe an irritated scalp and help reduce excess oil production.
Buds: High in antioxidants that can promote hair growth and improve hair health, while also helping to balance oil production.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of silver birch leaves, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes to allow the properties of the herb to infuse.
Turn off the heat, add 1 tablespoon of dried nettle leaves, and let it steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the solid parts, then let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Before shampooing, massage the prepared tea onto your scalp and throughout your hair, leaving it on for 15-20 minutes before rinsing.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily hair with herbal teas is by rinsing your hair with cooled tea made from chamomile, sage, or nettle. These herbs have astringent properties that help regulate sebum production, reducing oiliness. For optimal results, use twice a week after shampooing. Avoid over-brewing the tea, as this can lead to dryness and counteract the desired effect. Remember, individual results may vary, and it's essential to maintain a balanced diet and healthy lifestyle for overall hair health.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily hair that are treated with herbal teas include dandruff and an itchy scalp. Herbal teas like chamomile, nettle, and sage have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe an irritated scalp and reduce dandruff. These teas can be used as a rinse after shampooing or can be combined with other ingredients to create a hair mask. Additionally, green tea, which is rich in antioxidants, can help regulate oil production and promote hair growth. Regular use of these herbal teas can lead to a healthier scalp and shinier, more manageable hair.
